nodes.name: 气候变化
nodes.summary: 长期改变水文节律与生态系统稳定性的全球变化因素。

nodes.name: 农药污染
nodes.summary: 农业化学品通过水体和食物链影响鸟类生存。

nodes.name: 气候变暖
nodes.summary: 长期升温改变海冰和海洋环境条件。

nodes.name: 喜马拉雅山脉
nodes.summary: 喜马拉雅山脉是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联124种鸟类，包括斑头雁、琵嘴鸭、雪鹑等124种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 阿尔卑斯山
nodes.summary: 阿尔卑斯山是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括小嘴鸻、鵰鴞、黄嘴山鸦等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 黄嘴山鸦
nodes.summary: 黄嘴山鸦（Alpine chough，学名 Pyrrhocorax graculus），在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，主要分布于阿尔卑斯山、中亚、喜马拉雅山脉，常见栖息地包括峡谷峭壁，主要威胁包括气候变化、气候变暖、农药污染。

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 峡谷峭壁
nodes.summary: 大型猛禽和食腐鸟偏好的高崖栖息环境。

nodes.name: 中亚
nodes.summary: 中亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联34种鸟类，包括斑头雁、大鸨、小鸨等34种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|alt= high grey bare cliffs behind a meadow|Cliffs with nearby alpine pasture provide nesting sites. The Alpine chough breeds in mountains from Spain eastwards through southern Europe and the Alps across Central Asia and the Himalayas to western China

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|alt= high grey bare cliffs behind a meadow|Cliffs with nearby alpine pasture provide nesting sites. The Alpine chough breeds in mountains from Spain eastwards through southern Europe and the Alps across Central Asia and the Himalayas to western China

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: thumb|alt= high grey bare cliffs behind a meadow|Cliffs with nearby alpine pasture provide nesting sites. The Alpine chough breeds in mountains from Spain eastwards through southern Europe and the Alps across Central Asia and the Himalayas to western China

links.label: 栖息于
links.evidence: thumb|alt= high grey bare cliffs behind a meadow|Cliffs with nearby alpine pasture provide nesting sites. The Alpine chough breeds in mountains from Spain eastwards through southern Europe and the Alps across Central Asia and the Himalayas to western China

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Climate change may present a long-term threat, by shifting the necessary Alpine habitat to higher altitudes

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: 2|url=http://www.acta-zoologica-bulgarica.eu/downloads/acta-zoologica-bulgarica/2008/supplement-2-119-132.pdf | pages= 119–132}} Foraging habitat can also be lost to human activities such as the construction of ski resorts and other tourist development on former alpine meadows. Populations of choughs are stable or increasing in areas where traditional pastoral or other low intensity agriculture persists, but are declining or have become locally extinct where intensive farming methods have been introduced, such as Brittany, England, south-west Portugal and mainland Scotland. Choughs can be locally threatened by the accumulation of pesticides and heavy metals in the mountain soils, heavy rain, shooting and other human disturbances, but a longer-term threat comes from global warming, which would cause the species' preferred Alpine climate zone to shift to higher, more restricted areas, or locally to disappear entirely.{{cite journal | last= Sekercioglu | first= Cagan H | author2= Schneider, Stephen H

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: 2|url=http://www.acta-zoologica-bulgarica.eu/downloads/acta-zoologica-bulgarica/2008/supplement-2-119-132.pdf | pages= 119–132}} Foraging habitat can also be lost to human activities such as the construction of ski resorts and other tourist development on former alpine meadows. Populations of choughs are stable or increasing in areas where traditional pastoral or other low intensity agriculture persists, but are declining or have become locally extinct where intensive farming methods have been introduced, such as Brittany, England, south-west Portugal and mainland Scotland. Choughs can be locally threatened by the accumulation of pesticides and heavy metals in the mountain soils, heavy rain, shooting and other human disturbances, but a longer-term threat comes from global warming, which would cause the species' preferred Alpine climate zone to shift to higher, more restricted areas, or locally to disappear entirely.{{cite journal | last= Sekercioglu | first= Cagan H | author2= Schneider, Stephen H
